item.title: Republican Derek Merrin projected to win Ohio's 9th District primary
item.description: Derek Merrin won the GOP primary in Ohio's 9th Congressional District on Tuesday, setting up a rematch against longtime Democratic Rep. Marcy Kaptur in what's expected to be one of the most contested House races of the year.

item.title: 3 killed in U.S. strike on suspected drug boat in eastern Pacific, Pentagon says
item.description: The attack came a day after U.S. forces struck an alleged drug boat in the Caribbean Sea, killing two people.

item.title: Trump largely succeeds in upending Indiana state Senate over redistricting
item.description: President Trump had backed challengers to Indiana state senators who voted against his redistricting plan — and on Tuesday, most of them won.

item.title: Antisemitic graffiti found across Queens, New York
item.description: An investigation is underway in New York City after multiple locations were vandalized with antisemitic graffiti. CBS News' Jared Ochacher reports from Queens.

item.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire holds for now, Hegseth says, as Trump looks to finalize deal
item.description: Hegseth said the ceasefire with Iran "certainly holds" for now after a naval clash between Iranian and U.S. forces and renewed attacks on the UAE.

item.title: Suspect arrested after two fatal Carrollton shootings tied to business dispute, police say
item.description: Police say both shootings were connected and stemmed from a financial dispute between the suspect and the victims.

item.title: OpenAI co-founder's diary surfaces in Musk trial
item.description: The high-stakes trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI now involves testimony about a co-founder's personal diary. Vanity Fair contributor Tom Dotan joins to discuss.

item.title: "The Lost Boys," "Schmigadoon!" cinch 12 Tony noms each
item.description: "The Lost Boys," starring Shoshana Bean, leads this year's Tony nominations along with "Schmigadoon!" Both opened just weeks ago, and each cinched 12 nods. CBS News New York reporter Dave Carlin has more about the nominees.

item.title: 5/5: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Rubio says Operation Epic Fury is over; primary day in Indiana and Ohio.

item.title: Homan: "Things weren't perfect" in Minneapolis, but ICE not backing down
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan conceded "things weren't perfect" during the crackdown in Minneapolis, but stressed that the administration is not backing down from its mass deportation effort.

item.title: Sister of sailor on USS Gerald R. Ford discusses her deployment: "That's the life of the military"
item.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, the Navy's largest aircraft carrier, will soon return to the U.S. after the longest deployment since the Vietnam War. Among the returning sailors is Aviation Boatswain's Mate Airman Tiffany Galarza. Her sister, Brianna Caudill, joins "The Daily Report."

item.title: What to know about Tuesday's primaries in Ohio and Indiana
item.description: Voters headed to the polls on Tuesday in Ohio and Indiana — in the Buckeye State, they cast ballots in Senate, House and governor's primary races, while Hoosiers are weighing in on House and state races.

item.title: Manhunt on for retired special forces soldier accused of shooting wife
item.description: Authorities say Craig Berry, a retired special forces soldier, shot his wife and then fled into the woods in northern Tennessee.

item.title: Retired Special Forces soldier accused of shooting wife, disappearing in woods
item.description: In northern Tennessee, a retired Special Forces soldier is accused of shooting his wife and then using his training to disappear into the woods. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Michigan teacher steps up during talent show trouble
item.description: On this National Teacher Appreciation Day, Tony Dokoupil has the story of how a Michigan teacher stepped in to help a student facing a challenge at the talent show.

item.title: Trump pauses Project Freedom in Strait of Hormuz to see if Iran deal can be struck
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he has paused Project Freedom, a U.S. effort to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy projected to win Ohio GOP nomination for governor
item.description: Vivek Ramswamy will face Democrat Amy Acton, a former Ohio Department of Public Health director, in November.

item.title: Ohio Senate race set between Jon Husted, Sherrod Brown
item.description: CBS News projected that former Sen. Sherrod Brown won the Democratic primary, while incumbent Sen. Jon Husted ran unopposed.

item.title: Trump pausing Project Freedom for short period
item.description: President Trump announced Tuesday night that he's pausing Project Freedom for "a short period of time" to see if an agreement with Iran can be finalized.

item.title: In Lynette Hooker probe, Coast Guard seeks info about mystery sailboat
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is trying to find the owners of a sailboat that may have been moored next to Lynette Hooker's vessel the night she disappeared in the Bahamas, marking a new development in the search for the missing Michigan woman.

item.title: Former Georgia Lt. Gov. Geoff Duncan slams 2020 election probe as "all about fear and intimidation"
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking the names of people who worked the 2020 election in Fulton County, Georgia. Former Georgia GOP Lt. Gov. Geoff Duncan, who is running for governor as a Democrat, joins "The Takeout" with his reaction.

item.title: Here's why U.S. debt is surpassing GDP for the first time since World War II
item.description: For the first time since World War II, the U.S. national debt has surpassed the size of its economy. CBS News MoneyWatch associate managing editor Aimee Picchi has more.

item.title: Tom Homan says Trump administration is using "smarter enforcement" in Minneapolis
item.description: ICE and Border Patrol have come under intense public scrutiny over their immigration enforcement tactics, particularly in Minneapolis. Following the shooting deaths of two U.S. citizens by immigration agents in January, the Trump administration sent Border Czar Tom Homan to work with local officials. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez sat down exclusively with Homan.

item.title: Trump backing 7 Indiana Republicans looking to oust GOP lawmakers who voted against gerrymandering
item.description: Republicans are keeping a close eye on Ohio and Indiana on Tuesday as voters cast their ballots in state and congressional primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez has all the key details.

item.title: 5/5: CBS Evening News
item.description: Rare form of hantavirus believed to have spread person-to-person on cruise ship; Coast Guard seeks public's help in finding missing woman in Bahamas.

item.title: Rep. Johnny Olszewski proposes bill for 18-year Supreme Court term limits
item.description: Recent polling released by NBC News shows that only 22% of Americans have confidence in the U.S. Supreme Court. In the wake of that growing unpopularity, a new proposal in Congress would amend the Constitution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices. Democratic Rep. Johnny Olszewski of Maryland joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: To avoid risk of mines, Navy directs ships on path farther from Iran
item.description: The U.S. warns that transiting the normal route could be "extremely hazardous" because of mines laid in the Strait of Hormuz by Iran.

item.title: Iran war will worsen food insecurity in already fragile nations, humanitarian group warns
item.description: The aid organization Mercy Corps is warning that the Iran war will have devastating consequences for nations already on the brink of a humanitarian crisis. Kate Phillips-Barrasso, vice president for global policy and advocacy at Mercy Corps, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Utz potato chips sold nationwide recalled over salmonella risk
item.description: Utz Quality Foods is recalling some of its Zapp's and Dirty brand potato chips because an ingredient may be contaminated by salmonella.

item.title: See the best looks of the Met Gala 2026
item.description: Rihanna, Sabrina Carpenter, Madonna, and Beyoncé all wore their best looks to celebrate the "Costume Art" theme at the 2026 Met Gala. Here are some of the best moments, photos, and more from the night.
